# UC-22.2.3 ¬∑ NIS2 Vulnerability Disclosure and Patch Management Tracking

## Description

Surfaces active Tenable-plugin findings where the time since first_seen exceeds policy-based remediation deadlines per severity bucket for each host.

## Value

Compliance teams can hand auditors a repeatable report that shows which servers breached internal fix deadlines and for how long, instead of exporting ad-hoc CSV snapshots from the scanner console every quarter.

## Implementation

(1) Install Tenable Add-On (4060) and confirm events land in `index=vulnerability` with `state` populated. (2) Open **Settings ‚Üí Data Summary ‚Üí Sourcetypes** and verify whether your feed uses `first_seen` vs `first_found`. (3) Copy SLA constants into a macro `nis2_sla_severity(1)` so operations can change thresholds once. (4) For patch proof, mirror SCCM (1599) compliance into `index=patch` and schedule a follow-on correlation search. (5) Route alerts to SecOps on-call with the host CMDB owner field.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060) on a heavy forwarder or SC4S path that preserves JSON from Tenable.io / Security Center exports; search heads need the same TA version for field knowledge bundles.
‚Ä¢ Indexes: standardize on `index=vulnerability` for scanner-native events (rename if you still use `index=vuln`). Analyst role must include it.
‚Ä¢ Optional: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SCCM (Splunkbase 1599) writing to `index=patch` so patch compliance can be joined later.
‚Ä¢ Clock skew under **5 minutes** NTP between scanner and Splunk; otherwise `age_days` misstates SLA breaches.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Enable the Tenable modular input or API poller documented in the TA README; confirm `/services/data/inputs` REST endpoint lists the input as enabled. Set sourcetype override to `tenable:vuln` if the TA default matches your deployment build.
Validate daily volume: `index=vulnerability sourcetype=tenable:vuln earliest=-1d | stats count` ‚Äî compare with Tenable UI licensed asset counts.
For hybrid SCCM corroboration, route `sourcetype=sccm:compliance` with fields `BulletinID`, `ComplianceState`, `ComputerName` into `index=patch`.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the SPL with **Last 7 days** first to verify volume, then tighten to recurring schedule: **hourly** with **relative_time** window matching corporate SLA policy. Save as `UC-22.2.3_tenable_sla_breach`.
Add macro `%(nis2_sla_eval)` wrapping the `eval sla_days` block for reuse in executive dashboards.
If using CIM acceleration, mirror the breach logic inside `tstats` using summariesonly=true against `Vulnerabilities` datamodel ‚Äî expect 10x faster scans for 50k+ assets.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Pick one host flagged Critical in Tenable Workbench; compare `first_found` epoch in Splunk (`| convert ctime(first_found)`) to the UI timeline.
Run `| stats count by state` to ensure inactive findings are excluded when `state!="Active"`.
For SCCM cross-check, search `index=patch` for the same KB identifier referenced by the plugin family; mismatches indicate ticketing-not-patch issues, not scanner error.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Dashboard rows: host owner from CMDB lookup, business criticality, CAB-linked change URL. Alert actions: ServiceNow incident via scripted output or SOAR webhook.
Weekly managerial panel: SLA breach backlog trend by severity bucket; export CSV to `index=audit_evidence` through HEC if your GRC team requires immutable copies.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **Empty index** ‚Äî modular input credentials expired; rotate API keys in **Configuration > Accounts** of the TA.
‚Ä¢ **all age_days null** ‚Äî `_time` used instead of epoch field; confirm Tenable JSON path in `props.conf` `TIME_PREFIX`.
‚Ä¢ **over-counting breaches** ‚Äî duplicate events per scan job; add `| dedup plugin_id, host` before `stats`.
‚Ä¢ **CIM path slower than raw** ‚Äî acceleration disabled; enable under **Settings ‚Üí Data models ‚Üí Vulnerabilities** for at least 30 days summary range.
‚Ä¢ **False severity spikes** ‚Äî Tenable severity mapping changed; rebuild KV store reference table `tenable_severity_map.csv`.

## SPL

```spl
index=vulnerability sourcetype="tenable:vuln" state="Active"
| eval host=coalesce(host, hostname, dns_name)
| eval first_found=coalesce(first_found, first_seen)
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_found)/86400, 1)
| eval sla_days=case(severity="Critical",7, severity="High",30, 1=1,90)
| eval sla_breach=if(age_days>sla_days, 1, 0)
| stats count as open_vulns, max(age_days) as max_age by host, severity
| where sla_breach=1
| sort - max_age
| table host, severity, open_vulns, max_age, sla_days
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.severity | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table of hosts past SLA, bar chart of open_vulns by severity, line chart of rolling count of breached hosts over 90 days.
